title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Independent Server Trial
summary: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of an MCP security test suite and operating policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Treat external resource and tool content as untrusted data that cannot redefine authority.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Security Operations applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Patch, rotate, revoke, audit, rate-limit, and incident-test MCP deployments.
